page.content: In evaluating the associations between various risk factors and health outcomes, Model 1 provides a foundational reference point for understanding the odds ratios (OR) and their respective 95% confidence intervals (CI). In this model, the reference category is established at an OR of 1.00 for the non-disease (ND) group, serving as a baseline for comparison against other conditions.

page.content: The subsequent OR values presented in the table indicate the likelihood of health outcomes associated with specific risk factors, with the corresponding p-values offering insight into the statistical significance of these relationships. For instance, an OR greater than 1.00 suggests an increased likelihood of the outcome occurring in the presence of certain variables, while an OR less than 1.00 implies a protective effect. The 95% CI further elucidates the precision of these estimates, as a narrower interval indicates greater confidence in the OR. The p-values allow for the assessment of whether the observed associations are statistically significant, with values below 0.05 typically indicating a significant relationship. Together, these metrics facilitate a comprehensive understanding of the impact of various risk factors on health outcomes, guiding future research and potential interventions.
